data.title: Lumen Technologies snaps seven straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Lumen Technologies (NYSE:LUMN) shares snapped seven straight sessions of losses on Wednesday, as the stock closed 4.7% higher to $4.02.

The company lost about 20% in the preceding seven sessions. The stock has lost more than 25% in the last one year, compared to the over 4% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

LUMN is down 15% over the past one month. The stock closed 2% lower on Tuesday at $3.84.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, LUMN has a Hold rating with a score of 3.30 out of 5. The company received A+ for momentum, while it got a C- in the prospect of growth.

Turning to the Wall Street community, two analysts gave LUMN a Strong Buy. Nine analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

Last week, Bloomberg News reported that U.S. telecom AT&T (T) is in discussions to buy the consumer fiber business of Lumen Technologies and the deal, which has not been finalized, could be valued at over $5.5B.

A recent Seeking Alpha analyst by The Asian Investor said if “Lumen can reduce its leverage and pay down its formidable debt, shares could be ripe for an upside revaluation.”

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses on Wednesday, as the stock closed 3% to $34.18.

The Switzerland-based biotech company lost about 19% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has lost more than 13% in the last one year, compared to the over 4% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

CRSP is down 21.5% over the past one month. The stock closed 2.6% lower on Tuesday at $33.14.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, CRSP has a Hold rating with a score of 2.75 out of 5. The score was dragged down as the stock received C for momentum and valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 17 analysts gave CRSP a Buy and above. 10 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and one recommended Strong Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

“Upcoming milestones and updates on CRISPR Therapeutics is currently sitting at historical support, at a valuation that is clinical assets are expected to drive shareholder value back to previous highs,” argued a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

The stock, in February, was upgraded by Evercore on upcoming catalysts, with the brokerage saying that it was now including the CTX320 and CTX310 in vivo programs in its price target, with probabilities of success of 10% and 5%, respectively.

data.title: Idacorp raised to Buy at Jefferies on industry-leading ratebase CAGR, higher ROE
data.description:
Idacorp (NYSE:IDA) +1.3% in Wednesday's trading as Jefferies upgrades the Idaho-based electricity holding company to Buy from Hold with a $133 price target, up from $118 previously, citing confidence in management's ability to execute its growth strategy, implement regulatory initiatives and improve returns.

Analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith points to robust load growth, double-digit rate base compound annual growth rate, constructive regulation, unique rate structure, effective cost management, and solid track record of regulatory, financial and operational execution as positives for the company.

Idacorp's (NYSE:IDA) 2025 integrated resource plan 8.3% growth compared to 7.7% last year is a sign of acceleration that can support even more investment opportunities than under the base plan, the analyst says, also noting another prospective data center that would be additive, driving long-term growth in 2029 and beyond.

Idacorp (NYSE:IDA) is executing its infrastructure growth strategy while preserving the balance sheet, and the company takes pride in the simplicity of its cap structure - with no holdco debt - another differentiating fundamental compared to peers, Dumoulin-Smith says.

data.title: Imported canned beers are slapped with a 25% tariff as consumers brace for higher prices
data.description:
The Trump Administration has imposed a 25% tariff on all imported beer, according to a notice posted by the Commerce Department. The tariff is set to take effect at 12:01 a.m. EDT on Friday, April 4. The tariff applies to beer made from malt and empty aluminum cans of less than 20 liters. However, the tariff notice does not explicitly mention beer imported in glass containers.

The tariff is part of a broader aluminum derivative tariff policy and is expected to significantly impact the beer import market, particularly brands like Constellation Brands' (NYSE:STZ) Modelo and Corona, which are heavily reliant on canned packaging.

Other major beer importers into the U.S. for certain brands include Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY), Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Diageo (DEO), Molson Coors (TAP), Mark Anthony Brands, FIFCO, Asahi Group, and Pabst Brewing.

Analysts have warned that beer companies are likely to raise prices due to the tariff battle being fought on various fronts. In some cases, domestic beers could see higher prices as well due to underlying inflation pressure caused by the tariff scenario.

However, the beer sector has seen sluggish share price moves this year due to concerns that pricing won't recapture all the tariff costs or that volume will suffer. The sector also faces headwinds from consumer discretionary spending, commodity inflation, supply chain problems, and escalating trade issues.

data.title: Upstart set to snap six days of losses
data.description:
Shares of Upstart Holdings (NASDAQ:UPST) were on track to snap six straight days of losses, as the stock rose more than 2.7% to $47.26 in Wednesday afternoon trade. 

The California-based consumer lending company lost more than 16% in the last six trading sessions. The stock has lost more than 25% so far this year, compared to an over 4% decline in the broader S&P 500 Index.

UPST is down 21% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.07% down, at $46 on Tuesday. 

Morgan Stanley last to last month upgraded Upstart Holdings to Equal-weight from Underweight, encouraged by a favorable environment from both a funding and macro perspective for the lending platform after the company issued robust Q4 earnings and introduced strong 2025 guidance. 

The Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system has graded the stock a STRONG BUY, while Seeking Alpha authors and sell-side analysts see the stock as a HOLD. 

Seeking Alpha analyst and Investing Group leader Julian Lin noted in his recent analysis that Upstart has shown strong execution, with improved underwriting algorithms boosting top-line growth.

“The company has seen revenue growth accelerate and management expects further acceleration into 2025. This acceleration in growth is expected to coincide with a return to GAAP profitability,” he added.

data.title: Affluence Corporation to acquire Mingothings SL; appoints William Gonyer as CFO
data.description:
• Affluence Corporation (OTCPK:AFFU) on Wednesday announced the signing of a definitive agreement to acquire Mingothings SL (MTI), a specialist in integrated IoT solutions and data analytics for smart cities and connected industries.
  • Once the acquisition is completed, MTI, based in Barcelona, will work in partnership with OneMind Technologies ("OMT"), also a wholly owned subsidiary of Affluence.
  • Pursuant to the acquisition agreement, the outstanding equity of MTI will be exchanged by the shareholders of MTI for shares of preferred stock of Affluence Corporation, providing the former MTI shareholders with voting control of Affluence Corporation.
  • The company said the final step in the acquisition process is the customary notarization of the executed definitive agreement pursuant to the laws of Spain, expected to take place this month.
  • Affluence also announced the appointment of William E. Gonyer to the position of Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arcutis and Padagis to pause patent lawsuit over Zoryve
data.description:
Arcutis Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:ARQT) shares rose on Wednesday after Padagis and its units requested a pause in an ongoing patent lawsuit related to ARQT's lead product, Zoryve, and both parties agreed to temporarily halt the case. 

As part of the agreement, Padagis must inform Arcutis of any communication from the U.S. FDA regarding its Abbreviated New Drug Application for a potential generic version of Zoryve cream 0.3%, a treatment marketed for plaque psoriasis in adult and pediatric patients 6 years of age and older.

Additionally, the companies have agreed to extend the 30-month regulatory approval delay granted under the Hatch-Waxman Act.

The extension will add one extra day for each day the litigation remains paused. If the case resumes in the future, Arcutis will still benefit from the full regulatory stay period.

Patent protection for ZORYVE remains in place until at least 2037, the company said.

Stock is up about 10.5%.

data.title: ED, EXC, AWK top chart as Utilities services gainers in Q1; EIX, SRE, PCG among losers
data.description:
Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLU), which tracks S&P 500 Utilities services sector was one of the of top performing sector during the quarter with a gain of 3.38%, outperforming the broader S&P500 Index, which declined over 4% during the same period.

The utilities service sector, which includes companies like NextEra Energy (NEE), The Southern Company (SO), Duke Energy (DUK), American Electric Power (AEP), is one of the sectors other than energy (XLE), health (XLV) and consumer staples (XLP) that has outpaced the benchmark index during the quarter.

Strong quarterly performance helped stocks like Consolidated Edison (ED), Exelon (EXC) and American Water Works (AWK) to rise 24%, 22% and 19%, respectively, during the quarter.

According to FactSet analyst John Butters, the utilities sector is expected to report the third-highest (year-over-year) earnings growth rate of all eleven sectors at 9.9%.

Industries Q1 Performance

Within the index, Electric Utilities gained 3.63%, Multi-Utilities increased 5.22%, Water Utilities surged over 19%, while Independent Power Producers plunged nearly 19% in the first quarter of 2025.

At the industry level, according to FactSet report, all five industries are projected to report year-over-year earnings growth: Independent Power and Renewable Energy Producers (146%), Water Utilities (15%), Multi-Utilities (9%), Gas Utilities (6%), and Electric Utilities (6%).

U.S. stock fund flow in and out of the sector was average throughout the quarter, with some noticeable dips during the month of January and March. The Utilities services-focused ETF had a net inflow of $777.31 million, according to the data from etf.com.

What are the top 5 movers in Q1?

  • Gainers: Consolidated Edison (ED) +24.22%
  • Exelon (EXC) +22.36%                            
  • American Water Works (AWK) +19.10%      
  • American Electric Power (AEP) +18.85%
  • WEC Energy Group (WEC) +15.95%
  • Losers: Edison International (EIX) -26.36%              
  • Sempra (SRE) -18.10%                   
  • PG&E Corporation (PCG) -14.14%
  • Vistra (VST) -13.19%
  • Constellation Energy (CEG) -9.60%

What Quantitative Measures say?

XLU received a Buy rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, with a score of 4.25 out of 5. The sector got A+ for liquidity and dividend. However, it got a D- for the risk prospect.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious about the sector and rated it as a Hold.

What analysts expect?

Two analysts surveyed by Seeking Alpha in the last 90 days gave the XLU a Hold rating.

Seeking Alpha analyst Harrison Schwartz noted that XLU is among the few sector ETFs that I believe offer defensive potential in the current market environment with both inflation resurgence and cyclical risk factors.

However, Schwartz cautioned that growing bipartisan antitrust sentiment and utility lobbying regulations may hamper utilities' ability to raise rates in the long term.

data.title: Timken cut to Hold at Jefferies, as tariffs and sentiment push out H2 recovery
data.description:
Timken (NYSE:TKR) +1.7% in Wednesday's even as Jefferies downgrades shares of the manufacturer of bearings and power transmission products to Hold from Buy with a $70 price target, cut from $90, saying the potential for lower volumes and margin compression stemming from weakening short-cycle sentiment and potential tariff headwinds increases downside risk into H2.

Analyst Stephen Volkmann notes he upgraded Timken (NYSE:TKR) in December, optimistic that short-cycle markets would begin to turn higher in H2 on the back of deregulation and lower interest rates, but as sentiment has deteriorated, he believes consensus estimates likely will turn out to be too optimistic as PMIs have turned negative, metals prices are up and conversations with OEMs have turned more cautious on expectations that tariffs will hurt demand growth, adding that Timken lagged during the last bout of industry inflation despite strong end market demand, and pricing may be difficult in a slower demand environment.

Timken (NYSE:TKR) also is re-engaged in a CEO search after parting ways with Tarak Mehta after just six months; prior CEO Rich Kyle will step in as interim President and CEO.

data.title: AMC Entertainment on track to extend losses for seventh straight session
data.description:
AMC Entertainment (NYSE:AMC) continued to bleed for the seventh consecutive session as its shares declined 2.16% to trade at $2.72 on Friday afternoon.

Over the past six sessions, AMC shares lost over 9%. On Wednesday, they closed 3.14% lower at $2.78.

On a YTD basis, shares of movie theatre chain have declined over 30% while in the past year, it has seen a fall of 10%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, the stock has a Hold rating with a score of 2.68 out of 5. AMC has been rated a D for profitability and a D+ for valuation. Seeking Alpha as well and Wall Street analysts were also cautious about the company and recommended to Hold the stock. 

In March, financial services firm Wedbush said that it expects the North American box office to grow by at least 7% in 2025, with double-digit increases in the second and final quarters. Moreover, they’ve projected a growth of over 9% for 2026. But this has not helped in improving investors’ sentiment.

Seeking Alpha analyst Bill Maurer highlighted in an article earlier this week that AMC continues to face severe financial challenges post pandemic, including significant cash burn, high debt, and major dilution issues. The disappointing Q1 domestic box office results have further heightened concerns about the company's future revenue growth and cash flow, he said.

“While there has been a lot of optimism about the movie theater business this year, Q1 has gotten off to a tough start thanks to a dismal March. AMC Entertainment needs some strong growth in movie grosses to attempt to get its cash flow situation back to breakeven. The company started this year with large deficits in its working capital and shareholder's equity balances while trying to reduce a large debt pile,” Maurer added.
